summary: Store Manager 'Add Product' Button Visibility Bug - FIX VERIFIED. The Add Product button and Adjust Stock button are now correctly visible for store_manager role users on the Inventory page.

minor.issue: Returns 403 Forbidden for store_manager user because they don't have 'stores' permission in their permissions object
minor.note: This is expected behavior based on user's permission set, but causes 'Failed to load inventory' toast on Inventory page since it needs stores data for dropdowns

features_tested.details: Successfully logged in. User has permissions: {dashboard: true, inventory: true, items: true}
Successfully navigated to /inventory via sidebar nav item [data-testid='nav-inventory']
Add Product button is VISIBLE and clickable on the Inventory page for store_manager role. Button located at top-right with blue background (bg-blue-600)
Adjust Stock button is VISIBLE and clickable on the Inventory page for store_manager role. Button has data-testid='adjust-inventory-btn'
Clicking Add Product button navigates to Items page and opens 'Add Items to Inventory' modal with 'Add Manually' and 'Smart Stock Scanner' options
User can see the inventory page UI but 'Failed to load inventory' toast appears due to missing 'stores' permission

fix_verification.bug_description: Add Product button not visible for store_manager role user on Inventory page
fix_details: canPerformAction function now correctly handles store_manager role same as manager role
store_manager has access to items module create action since 'items' is in user permissions and not in restricted modules list
Restricted modules are: user_management, settings, backup_restore, billing
fix_verification.test_result: PASS - Add Product button is now visible and functional for store_manager role users

permission_flow_verified.reason: Line 307: store_manager is treated like manager, Line 308-311: 'items' not in restrictedModules list, Line 312: returns true

critical_code_review_comments: The fix in PermissionContext.js correctly treats store_manager role same as manager role for canPerformAction checks
Button visibility is correctly controlled by canPerformAction('items', 'create') on line 588-590 of InventoryPage.js
Minor UX issue: Inventory page depends on /api/stores which this user doesn't have permission for, causing error toast

success_rate.frontend: 100% - All tested features passed (Add Product button visible and functional)

context_for_next_testing_agent: The store_manager role 'Add Product' button visibility bug has been fixed and verified. The canPerformAction function in PermissionContext.js now correctly handles store_manager role. Test user: smartsalescorpbatmaloo@gmail.com / 12345678. Both Add Product and Adjust Stock buttons are visible and functional. Note: User gets 'Failed to load inventory' toast because they lack 'stores' permission, but this is a separate issue from the reported bug.
